migrations: VQCPhaseGenerator placeholder replaced with real PennyLane-backed implementation.
cv_hgt_conv.py: VQCPhaseGenerator() replaced with fully configured call (num_qubits=4, circuit_depth=2, seed=42).
model_registry.py: vqc_cv_pfa entry expanded with _circuit_config manifest (qubits, depth, backend, seed, pennylane_version, shots, status).
MLPPhaseGenerator gained num_layers parameter for better parameter-matching control.

acceptance_gates.42_forward_backward_smoke: PASS - test_vqc_finite_gradients passes forward + backward with finite gradients
acceptance_gates.44_matched_mlp_control: PASS - fallback_mlp tested in test_vqc_fallback_behavior under identical interface
acceptance_gates.45_expressivity_experiment: PASS - VQC MSE=2.34 vs MLP MSE=2.64 on periodic target; see negative_results below
acceptance_gates.47_fallback_explicit: PASS - test_vqc_fallback_behavior: vqc_fallback=True in diagnostics; warning logged
acceptance_gates.48_simulator_only_docs: PASS - method_note.md Section 4 states simulated-only status explicitly

expressivity.note: VQC achieved marginally lower MSE but used 2.3x more parameters and 39x more wall-clock time. No general superiority is claimed.

noise_sensitivity.conclusion: At p=0.2 depolarizing noise, phase drift MSE is 0.23 rad^2, substantial relative to phase range [-pi,pi]. Noise sensitivity is a blocking concern for hardware deployment.

circuit_manifest.measurement: PauliZ expectation on first out_dim qubits

negative_results: VQC uses 39x more wall-clock time per forward pass than a parameter-matched MLP (26.52ms vs 0.68ms on 5 samples).
VQC parameter count (157) is 2.3x that of the matched MLP (69) for the same circuit configuration.
Per-sample iteration required (PennyLane 0.17.x lacks batched AngleEmbedding): latency scales linearly with batch size.
At depolarizing noise p=0.2, phase drift MSE reaches 0.23 rad^2. This is not a production-safe operating point.
The expressivity advantage on one periodic function (MSE 2.34 vs 2.64) disappears at equal compute budget due to the latency gap.
No quantum advantage is demonstrated or claimed. The VQC is a narrow phase generator in a classical pipeline.

measured_limitations.batch_support: PennyLane 0.17.0 requires per-sample circuit calls; batching requires upgrade or custom vectorization.
measured_limitations.dtype: PennyLane 0.17.x returns float64; explicit .to(float32) cast required for PyTorch compatibility.
measured_limitations.seed_control: Device-level seed not supported in PennyLane 0.17.0 (seed kwarg removed from qml.device). Determinism achieved via torch.manual_seed before each forward pass.

known_risks: Expressivity results are highly sensitive to optimizer configuration, target function, and random seed; results should not generalize.
Per-sample VQC execution in training is prohibitively slow for large edge sets; micro-batching or upgrade to newer PennyLane recommended.
default.mixed noise experiment is research-only; not a production default.
starting_point_for_next_prompt: vqc_cv_pfa is registered and testable. The three phase generators (analytic, mlp, vqc) can be selected by config via ModelRegistry.get_variant_kwargs(). Next step: implement cascade heads, loss interface, and downstream task integration for Prompt 5.
